hero.title: Create realistic chat screenshots for Discord, WhatsApp, and Telegram
hero.description: Chat Simulator is a browser-based tool for building editable chat mockups with local-first uploads, message timing controls, and export options for both web and mobile layouts.
hero.tip: Images, avatars, and files are processed in the browser before export.
hero.avatars_tip: Built for creators, product demos, parody concepts, and mock conversation layouts

introduce.title: What Chat Simulator helps you do
introduce.label: Overview
introduce.description: Use Chat Simulator to create polished conversation mockups in your browser without design tools, image editors, or complex setup.

items.title: Match familiar chat layouts
items.description: Switch between Discord, WhatsApp, and Telegram-inspired layouts so your screenshots look closer to the platform people recognize.

items.title: Edit people, timing, and content
items.description: Control speaker names, avatars, message sides, timestamps, and attachments directly inside the preview.

items.title: Export for web or mobile
items.description: Generate screenshots in desktop-style or phone-style layout, then export as PNG or JPG when the conversation looks right.

items.title: Keep uploads local-first
items.description: Images, files, and avatars are prepared in the browser for editing and export instead of being turned into stored user chats.

benefits.title: Why people use Chat Simulator
benefits.label: Benefits
benefits.description: The tool is designed to be easy to understand, quick to edit, and suitable for harmless mockups, creative content, and product visuals.

items.title: Useful for mockups and content concepts
items.description: Create conversation visuals for social posts, product explainers, landing pages, and harmless parody screenshots.

items.title: Fast to tweak without leaving the browser
items.description: Adjust names, profile images, timestamps, backgrounds, and export settings from a single editing interface.

items.title: Clear privacy and policy pages
items.description: The site includes About, FAQ, Privacy, and Terms pages so visitors can review how the tool works and how site-level services are described.

usage.title: How to create a chat screenshot
usage.description: A simple workflow for building and exporting a conversation mockup:

items.title: Pick a chat style
items.description: Start with Discord, WhatsApp, or Telegram, or load one of the built-in funny example cases for a faster starting point.

items.title: Edit the conversation
items.description: Customize people, avatars, channel details, timestamps, images, and files until the mockup matches the scene you want.

items.title: Preview pacing
items.description: Use playback to see how messages appear in sequence before exporting the final image.

items.title: Export the final image
items.description: Choose PNG or JPG, then export in web or mobile layout depending on where you plan to use the screenshot.

features.title: Core features
features.description: These are the editing and export capabilities available in the current tool.

items.title: Platform-specific chat layouts
items.description: Use Discord, WhatsApp, and Telegram-inspired conversation views with native-feeling headers and message structure.

items.title: Editable chat identities
items.description: Change names, avatars, default message side, and per-message details without rebuilding the whole thread.

items.title: Image and file attachments
items.description: Add screenshots, images, or files into the chat flow to make examples feel more complete and realistic.

items.title: Web and mobile export modes
items.description: Generate screenshots that match a desktop composition or a phone-style viewport before export.

items.title: Local-first asset handling
items.description: Uploaded avatars, images, and files are prepared in the browser rather than treated like permanent user account data.

items.title: Fun case templates
items.description: Apply playful built-in scenarios, then remix them into your own custom conversation screenshots.

items.name: Product mockup

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Have another question? Review the policy pages or contact us by email.

items.question: What is Chat Simulator?
items.answer: Chat Simulator is a browser-based tool for creating mock chat screenshots in Discord, WhatsApp, and Telegram-style layouts. You can edit names, avatars, timestamps, attachments, and export settings from one interface.

items.question: Are uploaded images and files stored as user chats?
items.answer: The editing flow is designed to process uploaded avatars, images, and files in the browser first so you can preview and export mockups without turning them into persistent user chat histories.

items.question: Can I export both desktop-style and phone-style screenshots?
items.answer: Yes. The export panel supports both web-style and mobile-style layout modes, plus PNG or JPG output and multiple aspect ratios.

items.question: What can I customize in a conversation?
items.answer: You can change the chat title, people, avatars, timestamps, message side, backgrounds, images, files, and export settings. Some controls vary by platform layout.

items.question: Is this tool meant for real private messaging?
items.answer: No. The tool is designed for mockups, creative examples, demonstrations, and screenshot concepts rather than storing or replacing real private messaging services.

items.question: Where can I read more about policies and contact details?
items.answer: You can review the About, FAQ, Privacy, and Terms pages linked in the footer. The site contact email is also listed there.
